revision.comment: 「公智神社」についての新規記事
revision.text:
公智神社（くちじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県西宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式内社]]、旧[[県社]]。 [[健速須佐之男命]]・[[久久能智神]]・[[奇稲田姫命]]を祀る。
== 由緒 == 創建年月日は不詳であるが、[[孝元天皇]]の皇子[[大彦命]]の末裔[[久々智氏]]族が木の祖神久久能智神（句句廼馳神）を氏神として奉祀したのが始まりと伝えられている。大化3年冬、孝徳天皇有馬行幸の際の御用材を当社地より用い、その品質の良好なことを喜ばれ「功地山」の山名を賜る。貞観年中に健速須佐之男命・奇稲田姫命を合祀し、天王宮・祇園牛頭天王と称された。昭和4年4月に県社に加列。

revision.comment: スタブ・カテゴリ
revision.text:
'''公智神社'''（くちじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている式内社で、旧社格は[[県社]]。
[[スサノオ|健速須佐之男命]]・[[ククノチ|久久能智神]]・[[奇稲田姫命]]を祀る。
== 由緒 == 創建年月日は不詳であるが、[[孝元天皇]]の皇子[[大彦命]]の末裔[[久々智氏]]族が木の祖神久久能智神（句句廼馳神）を氏神として奉祀したのが始まりと伝えられている。大化3年冬、孝徳天皇有馬行幸の際の御用材を当社地より用い、その品質の良好なことを喜ばれ「功地山」の山名を賜る。貞観年中に健速須佐之男命・奇稲田姫命を合祀し、天王宮・祇園牛頭天王と称された。昭和4年4月に県社に加列。
==外部リンク== [http://kuchijinja.jp/ 公智神社]（公式サイト）

旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2日曜日 |神事 = |地図=Japan Hyogo}} '''公智神社'''（くちじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、旧社格は[[県社]]。 [[スサノオ|健速須佐之男命]]・[[ククノチ|久久能智神]]・[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]を祀る。 == 由緒 == 創建年月日は不詳であるが、[[孝元天皇]]の皇子[[大彦命]]の末裔[[久々智氏]]族が木の祖神久久能智神（句句廼馳神）を氏神として奉祀したのが始まりと伝えられている。大化3年冬、孝徳天皇有馬行幸の際の御用材を当社地より用い、その品質の良好なことを喜ばれ「功地山」の山名を賜る。貞観年中に健速須佐之男命・奇稲田姫命を合祀し、天王宮・祇園牛頭天王と称された。昭和4年4月に県社に加列。 == 外部リンク == *[http://kuchijinja.jp/ 公智神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くちしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]]

It was made by the descendants of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōhiko|lt=Prince Ōhiko|qq=}}

revision.comment: 19 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 公智神社 |画像 = [[File:Kuchi shrine, Nishinomiya.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]山口町下山口3-14-30 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[スサノオ|健速須佐之男命]]
[[ククノチ|久久能智神]]
[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2月曜日の前日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=公智神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''公智神社'''（くちじんじゃ、こうちじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、旧社格は[[県社]]。 [[ククノチ|久久能智神]]、[[スサノオ|健速須佐之男命]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]を祀る。 == 由緒 == 創建年月日は不詳であるが、[[孝元天皇]]の皇子[[大彦命]]の末裔[[久々智氏]]族が木の祖神久久能智神（句句廼馳神）を氏神として奉祀したのが始まりと伝えられている。大化3年冬、孝徳天皇有馬行幸の際の御用材を当社地より用い、その品質の良好なことを喜ばれ「功地山」の山名を賜る。[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]年中に健速須佐之男命・奇稲田姫命を合祀し、天王宮・祇園牛頭天王と称された。昭和4年4月に県社に加列。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ 公智神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くちしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]]

旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2月曜日の前日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=公智神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''公智神社'''（くちじんじゃ、こうちじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、旧社格は[[県社]]。 [[ククノチ|久久能智神]]、[[スサノオ|健速須佐之男命]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]を祀る。 == 由緒 == 創建年月日は不詳であるが、[[孝元天皇]]の皇子[[大彦命]]の末裔[[久々智氏]]族が木の祖神久久能智神（句句廼馳神）を氏神として奉祀したのが始まりと伝えられている。大化3年冬、孝徳天皇有馬行幸の際の御用材を当社地より用い、その品質の良好なことを喜ばれ「功地山」の山名を賜る。[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]年中に健速須佐之男命・奇稲田姫命を合祀し、天王宮・祇園牛頭天王と称された。昭和4年4月に県社に加列。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ 公智神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] ==End Japanese==

旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2月曜日の前日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=公智神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''公智神社'''（くちじんじゃ、こうちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|西宮市|Q243853}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記されている{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|県社}}。 {{jalink|ククノチ|Q10749920|久久能智神}}、{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|健速須佐之男命}}、{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|奇稲田姫命}}を祀る。 === 由緒 === 創建年月日は不詳であるが、{{jalink|孝元天皇|Q333572}}の皇子{{jalink|大彦命|Q11435420}}の末裔{{jalink|久々智氏}}族が木の祖神久久能智神（句句廼馳神）を氏神として奉祀したのが始まりと伝えられている。大化3年冬、孝徳天皇有馬行幸の際の御用材を当社地より用い、その品質の良好なことを喜ばれ「功地山」の山名を賜る。{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}年中に健速須佐之男命・奇稲田姫命を合祀し、天王宮・祇園牛頭天王と称された。昭和4年4月に県社に加列。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ 公智神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]]

旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2月曜日の前日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=公智神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''公智神社'''（くちじんじゃ、こうちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|西宮市|Q243853}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記されている{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|県社}}。 {{jalink|ククノチ|Q10749920|久久能智神}}、{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|健速須佐之男命}}、{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|奇稲田姫命}}を祀る。 === 由緒 === 創建年月日は不詳であるが、{{jalink|孝元天皇|Q333572}}の皇子{{jalink|大彦命|Q11435420}}の末裔{{jalink|久々智氏}}族が木の祖神久久能智神（句句廼馳神）を氏神として奉祀したのが始まりと伝えられている。大化3年冬、孝徳天皇有馬行幸の際の御用材を当社地より用い、その品質の良好なことを喜ばれ「功地山」の山名を賜る。{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}年中に健速須佐之男命・奇稲田姫命を合祀し、天王宮・祇園牛頭天王と称された。昭和4年4月に県社に加列。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ 公智神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]]

(Japan) (P3225) == * 2140005015993 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県西宮市山口町下山口3-14-30', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:公智神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11392062}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 公智神社 |画像 = [[File:Kuchi shrine, Nishinomiya.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|西宮市|Q243853}}山口町下山口3-14-30 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|健速須佐之男命}}
{{jalink|ククノチ|Q10749920|久久能智神}}
{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|奇稲田姫命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2月曜日の前日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=公智神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''公智神社'''（くちじんじゃ、こうちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|西宮市|Q243853}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記されている{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|県社}}。 {{jalink|ククノチ|Q10749920|久久能智神}}、{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|健速須佐之男命}}、{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|奇稲田姫命}}を祀る。 === 由緒 === 創建年月日は不詳であるが、{{jalink|孝元天皇|Q333572}}の皇子{{jalink|大彦命|Q11435420}}の末裔{{jalink|久々智氏}}族が木の祖神久久能智神（句句廼馳神）を氏神として奉祀したのが始まりと伝えられている。大化3年冬、孝徳天皇有馬行幸の際の御用材を当社地より用い、その品質の良好なことを喜ばれ「功地山」の山名を賜る。{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}年中に健速須佐之男命・奇稲田姫命を合祀し、天王宮・祇園牛頭天王と称された。昭和4年4月に県社に加列。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ 公智神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

[[File:Kuchi shrine, Nishinomiya.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|西宮市|Q243853}}山口町下山口3-14-30 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|健速須佐之男命}}
{{jalink|ククノチ|Q10749920|久久能智神}}
{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|奇稲田姫命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2月曜日の前日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=公智神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''公智神社'''（くちじんじゃ、こうちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|西宮市|Q243853}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記されている{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|県社}}。 {{jalink|ククノチ|Q10749920|久久能智神}}、{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|健速須佐之男命}}、{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|奇稲田姫命}}を祀る。 === 由緒 === 創建年月日は不詳であるが、{{jalink|孝元天皇|Q333572}}の皇子{{jalink|大彦命|Q11435420}}の末裔{{jalink|久々智氏}}族が木の祖神久久能智神（句句廼馳神）を氏神として奉祀したのが始まりと伝えられている。大化3年冬、孝徳天皇有馬行幸の際の御用材を当社地より用い、その品質の良好なことを喜ばれ「功地山」の山名を賜る。{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}年中に健速須佐之男命・奇稲田姫命を合祀し、天王宮・祇園牛頭天王と称された。昭和4年4月に県社に加列。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ 公智神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Worship hall |??? = {{jalink|Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Nishinomiya|Q243853}} Yamaguchi-ch? Shimoyamaguchi 3-14-30 |?? = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |?? = {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} |?? = |?? = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small shrine)
former {{jalink|Ken-sha}} |?? = unknown |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = the day before the second Monday of October |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kuchi Shrine''' (??????, ???????) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Nishinomiya|Q243853}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Ken-sha}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{jalink|Kukuchi clan}}, descendants of {{jalink|?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420}}, a prince of {{jalink|Emperor K?gen|Q333572}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{jalink|J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|J?gan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:??????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Worship hall |??? = {{jalink|Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Nishinomiya|Q243853}} Yamaguchi-ch? Shimoyamaguchi 3-14-30 |?? = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |?? = {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} |?? = |?? = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small shrine)
former {{jalink|Ken-sha}} |?? = unknown |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = the day before the second Monday of October |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kuchi Shrine''' (??????, ???????) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Nishinomiya|Q243853}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Ken-sha}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{jalink|Kukuchi clan}}, descendants of {{jalink|?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420}}, a prince of {{jalink|Emperor K?gen|Q333572}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{jalink|J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|J?gan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:??????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

During the {{jalink|J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|J?gan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:??????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Worship hall |??? = {{jalink|Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Nishinomiya|Q243853}} Yamaguchi-ch? Shimoyamaguchi 3-14-30 |?? = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |?? = {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} |?? = |?? = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small shrine)
former {{jalink|Ken-sha}} |?? = unknown |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = the day before the second Monday of October |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kuchi Shrine''' (??????, ???????) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Nishinomiya|Q243853}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Ken-sha}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{jalink|Kukuchi clan}}, descendants of {{jalink|?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420}}, a prince of {{jalink|Emperor K?gen|Q333572}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{jalink|J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|J?gan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:??????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]]

{{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} |?? = |?? = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small shrine)
former {{jalink|Ken-sha}} |?? = unknown |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = the day before the second Monday of October |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kuchi Shrine''' (??????, ???????) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Nishinomiya|Q243853}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Ken-sha}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Kukunochi|Q10749920|Kukunochi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{jalink|Kukuchi clan}}, descendants of {{jalink|?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420}}, a prince of {{jalink|Emperor K?gen|Q333572}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{jalink|J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|J?gan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:??????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:??????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Nnn]]

{{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} |?? = |?? = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ken-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} |?? = unknown |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = the day before the second Monday of October |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kuchi Shrine''' (??????, ???????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Nishinomiya|Q243853|WD=Q243853}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ken-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kukuchi clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, descendants of {{ill|lt=?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}}, a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor K?gen|Q333572|WD=Q333572}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=J?gan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:??????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Nnn]]

{{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} |?? = |?? = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small shrine)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} |?? = unknown |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = the day before the second Monday of October |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kuchi Shrine''' (??????, ???????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Nishinomiya|Q243853|WD=Q243853}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kukuchi clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, descendants of {{ill|lt=?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}}, a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor K?gen|Q333572|WD=Q333572}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=J?gan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:??????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Nnn]]

It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kukuchi clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, descendants of {{ill|lt=?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}}, a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor K?gen|Q333572|WD=Q333572}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=J?gan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:??????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Nnn]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} |?? = unknown |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = the day before the second Monday of October |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kuchi Shrine''' (??????, ???????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nishinomiya|Q243853|WD=Q243853|1=Nishinomiya}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Kami|1=Kukunochi}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kukuchi clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, descendants of {{ill|lt=?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor K?gen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=J?gan|1=Jōgan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:??????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Nnn]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} |?? = unknown |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = the day before the second Monday of October |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kuchi Shrine''' (??????, ???????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nishinomiya|Q243853|WD=Q243853|1=Nishinomiya}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Kami|1=Kukunochi}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kukuchi clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, descendants of {{ill|lt=?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor K?gen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=J?gan|1=Jōgan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:??????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]] [[Category:Nnn]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nishinomiya]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} |?? = unknown |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = the day before the second Monday of October |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kuchi Shrine''' (??????, ???????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nishinomiya|Q243853|WD=Q243853|1=Nishinomiya}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Kami|1=Kukunochi}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kukuchi clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, descendants of {{ill|lt=?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor K?gen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=J?gan|1=Jōgan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:??????}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nishinomiya]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} |?? = unknown |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = the day before the second Monday of October |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kuchi Shrine''' (??????, ???????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nishinomiya|Q243853|WD=Q243853|1=Nishinomiya}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Kami|1=Kukunochi}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kukuchi clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, descendants of {{ill|lt=?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor K?gen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=J?gan|1=Jōgan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nishinomiya]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} |?? = unknown |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = the day before the second Monday of October |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kuchi Shrine''' (??????, ???????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nishinomiya|Q243853|WD=Q243853|1=Nishinomiya}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Kami|1=Kukunochi}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kukuchi clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, descendants of {{ill|lt=?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor K?gen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=J?gan|1=Jōgan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nishinomiya]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} |?? = unknown |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = the day before the second Monday of October |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kuchi Shrine''' (??????, ???????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nishinomiya|Q243853|WD=Q243853|1=Nishinomiya}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Kami|1=Kukunochi}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kukuchi clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, descendants of {{ill|lt=?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor K?gen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=J?gan|1=Jōgan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nishinomiya, Hyogo]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} |?? = unknown |?? = |?? = |??? = |?? = the day before the second Monday of October |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kuchi Shrine''' (??????, ???????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hy?go Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nishinomiya|Q243853|qid=Q243853|1=Nishinomiya}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Sh?sha|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|qid=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Kami|1=Kukunochi}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|qid=Q505469|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but tradition says it began when the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kukuchi clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, descendants of {{ill|lt=?biko no Mikoto|Q11435420|qid=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}, a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor K?gen|Q333572|qid=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, worshipped the ancestral deity of trees Kukunochi-no-Kami (Kukunohachi-no-Kami) as their tutelary deity. In the winter of Taika 3, timber from this shrine's area was used for imperial works during Emperor K?toku's visit to Arima, and because of its excellent quality the mountain name "K?chiyama" was bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=J?gan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=J?gan|1=Jōgan}} era, Takesoku Susanoo-no-Mikoto and Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto were jointly enshrined, and the shrine was called Tenn?-g? and Gion Gozu Tenn?. In April 1929, it was promoted to prefectural shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://kuchijinja.jp/ Kuchi Shrine]?official site? {{?? ?}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuchi Shrine]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:西宮市の建築物]] [[Category:西宮市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:山口地域]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nishinomiya, Hyogo]]
